posts.title: When Should a Founder Hire a COO? (And Why It's Not When You Think)
posts.excerpt: Most founders wait until they're drowning to hire a COO. But the best time isn't when everything is broken — it's when you catch yourself becoming the thing that slows everyone else down.
posts.primary_keyword: when should a founder hire a COO

posts.title: What Is a Fractional COO? A CEO's Guide to Getting Unstuck
posts.excerpt: A Fractional COO isn't just a part-time employee. They're a strategic operating partner who helps founders build the systems and clarity needed to scale—without the overhead of a full-time C-suite hire.

posts.title: How to Get Your Leadership Team Aligned (Without Over-Engineering It)
posts.excerpt: Your leadership team keeps having the same conversations without reaching decisions. Here's what I learned about real alignment after years of meetings that went nowhere.
posts.primary_keyword: how do I get my leadership team aligned

posts.title: What Systems Does a 10-Person Team Actually Need?
posts.excerpt: You don't need enterprise-level processes when you're 10 people. But you do need some structure. Here's what actually works—and what doesn't—when you're building systems for a growing team.
posts.primary_keyword: what systems does a 10-person team actually need

posts.title: The Real Cost of Being the Founder Bottleneck
posts.excerpt: You're working all the time and it's stressing the team out. That feedback hit harder than any performance metric ever could. Here's what I learned about being the founder bottleneck.
